cleaned_narrative:
The candidate is a Mulesoft Developer with approximately 2.5 years of professional experience, specializing in API integration, web service development, and database interaction using the Mulesoft Anypoint Platform, Java, and related technologies. Their expertise lies in designing and implementing RESTful APIs, performing complex Dataweave transformations, and working with various database systems.

This candidate is not a suitable fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job requires a minimum of 5 years of experience specifically in automotive embedded software development, with expert-level proficiency in Embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, ISO 26262 functional safety, and Automotive SPICE processes. The candidate's profile demonstrates no experience in any of these critical areas, nor in the automotive or embedded systems domain.

Given the complete mismatch in core technical requirements, industry domain, and required experience level, this candidate does not meet the fundamental qualifications for the position.
key_strengths: API Integration Expertise:** Proven experience in Mulesoft development, Anypoint Platform, designing RAML-based APIs, and integrating various third-party APIs (Microsoft, Google, LinkedIn, Monster, GreytHr, Jira).
Data Transformation Skills:** Strong skills in complex Dataweave (DWL) transformations and utilization of various connectors.
Mentorship Experience:** "Mentored junior developers to improve their technical skills, fostering a culture of continuous learning within the team."
Database Proficiency:** Hands-on experience with Postgres, MS-SQL, and MySQL databases.
potential_concerns: Domain and Core Technology Mismatch:** The most significant gap is the complete lack of experience in automotive embedded software development, C/C++, AUTOSAR, automotive microcontrollers, CAN/CAN-FD, ISO 26262, and Automotive SPICE. These are all mandatory requirements for the role, and their absence makes the candidate fundamentally unqualified.
Experience Level:** The candidate has approximately 2.5 years of experience, which is significantly less than the 5+ years of *automotive embedded* experience required for a senior role.

Embedded Systems Knowledge:** There is no evidence of experience with hardware interaction, real-time operating systems, low-level programming, or debugging embedded systems, which are fundamental to an embedded software engineer role.
detailed_analysis:
SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
The candidate is a Mulesoft Developer with approximately 2.5 years of professional experience, specializing in API integration, web service development, and database interaction using the Mulesoft Anypoint Platform, Java, and related technologies. Their expertise lies in designing and implementing RESTful APIs, performing complex Dataweave transformations, and working with various database systems.

This candidate is not a suitable fit for the Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. The job requires a minimum of 5 years of experience specifically in automotive embedded software development, with expert-level proficiency in Embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, Infineon Aurix microcontrollers, ISO 26262 functional safety, and Automotive SPICE processes. The candidate's profile demonstrates no experience in any of these critical areas, nor in the automotive or embedded systems domain.

Given the complete mismatch in core technical requirements, industry domain, and required experience level, this candidate does not meet the fundamental qualifications for the position.

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
Total Experience:
JD_REQUIRES: 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience.
CANDIDATE_HAS: Approximately 2.5 years (07/2021 - Present) in Mulesoft development.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Core Programming Languages:
JD_REQUIRES: Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++.
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Sound knowledge of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) Patterns and Concepts of Java Programming." and "Developed Java code". No mention of C or C++.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Primary Domain / Industry:
JD_REQUIRES: Automotive embedded software development, electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain.
CANDIDATE_HAS: Supply chain (Midas), Intranet systems (Odyssey), device data sanitization (Erasure). No mention of automotive or embedded systems.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Frameworks & Tools:
JD_REQUIRES: Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience, Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers, CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise, Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools.
CANDIDATE_HAS: Mulesoft Anypoint Platform (Anypoint Studio, Mule 3.X/4.X), RAML, Dataweave, Postgres, MS-SQL, MySQL, MUnit, Postman, React JS. No mention of any automotive-specific frameworks or tools.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Architecture & Design Patterns:
JD_REQUIRES: AUTOSAR architecture design and integration.
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Sound knowledge of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) Patterns and Concepts of Java Programming." and experience with API design (RAML-based APIs, Restful APIs). No mention of AUTOSAR.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Cloud / Infrastructure:
JD_REQUIRES: Not explicitly mentioned, but embedded roles often involve on-device rather than cloud.
CANDIDATE_HAS: "API deployment to on-premises environments".
MATCH_STATUS: NOT_SPECIFIED

Leadership & Team Management:
JD_REQUIRES: Team leadership or technical architect experience, mentor junior engineers.
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Mentored junior developers to improve their technical skills, fostering a culture of continuous learning within the team."
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH

Education / Certifications:
JD_REQUIRES: ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification (Nice to Have).
CANDIDATE_HAS: Not specified.
MATCH_STATUS: NOT_SPECIFIED

Functional Safety & Quality Standards:
JD_REQUIRES: ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application, Automotive SPICE process experience, MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise.
CANDIDATE_HAS: No mention of ISO 26262, Automotive SPICE, or MISRA C/C++.
MATCH_STATUS: NO_MATCH

Requirements Engineering:
JD_REQUIRES: Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer.
CANDIDATE_HAS: "Ability to prepare comprehensive documentation, including API specifications and technical design documents." No mention of specific RE tools.
MATCH_STATUS: PARTIAL_MATCH

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
-   **API Integration Expertise:** Proven experience in Mulesoft development, Anypoint Platform, designing RAML-based APIs, and integrating various third-party APIs (Microsoft, Google, LinkedIn, Monster, GreytHr, Jira).
-   **Data Transformation Skills:** Strong skills in complex Dataweave (DWL) transformations and utilization of various connectors.
-   **Mentorship Experience:** "Mentored junior developers to improve their technical skills, fostering a culture of continuous learning within the team."
-   **Database Proficiency:** Hands-on experience with Postgres, MS-SQL, and MySQL databases.

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
-   **Domain and Core Technology Mismatch:** The most significant gap is the complete lack of experience in automotive embedded software development, C/C++, AUTOSAR, automotive microcontrollers, CAN/CAN-FD, ISO 26262, and Automotive SPICE. These are all mandatory requirements for the role, and their absence makes the candidate fundamentally unqualified.
-   **Experience Level:** The candidate has approximately 2.5 years of experience, which is significantly less than the 5+ years of *automotive embedded* experience required for a senior role.
-   **Embedded Systems Knowledge:** There is no evidence of experience with hardware interaction, real-time operating systems, low-level programming, or debugging embedded systems, which are fundamental to an embedded software engineer role.

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: 2.5 years
Seniority Level: MID
Core Technical Skills: Mulesoft, Anypoint Platform, Anypoint Studio, Mule 3.X/4.X, Restful APIs, RAML, Dataweave, Java, Postgres, MS-SQL, MySQL.
Secondary Skills: MUnit, Postman, React JS, OOP Concepts, API documentation, Error handling, Logger, File connectors, Scheduler.
Primary Domain: API Integration, Supply Chain, Intranet Systems.
Leadership Indicators: "Mentored junior developers to improve their technical skills".

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
REVIEW
Confidence: 10%
Match Score: 5%

Reason: The candidate's profile demonstrates expertise as a Mulesoft Developer with approximately 2.5 years of experience, which is entirely misaligned with the mandatory requirements for a Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer role. There is a complete absence of experience in embedded C/C++, AUTOSAR, automotive microcontrollers, functional safety (ISO 26262), and automotive processes (SPICE), making this candidate unsuitable for the position. The experience level is also significantly below the minimum requirement.

llm_prompt_used:
You are a senior technical recruiter performing a detailed fitment analysis. Compare the anonymized professional profile against the job description below.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- The CV is already anonymized (all PII removed). NEVER output names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, or company locations.
- If you cannot determine something, state "Not specified" — NEVER invent details.
- Be thorough and evidence-based. Cite specific technologies, years, and project details from the CV.
- Provide a DETAILED category-by-category comparison like a professional recruitment report.
- The Overall Assessment MUST be an original analytical summary you write — do NOT copy-paste text from the CV.

OUTPUT FORMAT (FOLLOW THIS STRUCTURE EXACTLY):

SECTION 1 – Overall Assessment:
[Write a 2-3 paragraph ORIGINAL executive summary in your own words covering:
- Who the candidate is (years of experience, primary role, key expertise areas)
- Why they are / are not a good fit for this specific role
- One line recommendation
IMPORTANT: This must be YOUR analytical summary, NOT copied text from the CV. Synthesize the information into a professional recruiter assessment.]

SECTION 2 – Fitment Analysis Table:
For EACH major requirement category in the JD, provide a line in this exact format:
CATEGORY: [category name]
JD_REQUIRES: [what the JD asks for]
CANDIDATE_HAS: [what the candidate actually has, with evidence]
MATCH_STATUS: [FULL_MATCH | PARTIAL_MATCH | NO_MATCH]

(Create one entry for each of these categories, adapting to the JD:
- Total Experience
- Core Programming Languages
- Primary Domain / Industry
- Frameworks & Tools
- Architecture & Design Patterns
- Cloud / Infrastructure
- Leadership & Team Management
- Education / Certifications
- Any other JD-specific categories)

SECTION 3 – Key Strengths:
- [Strength 1 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 2 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 3 with specific evidence from CV]
- [Strength 4 with specific evidence from CV (if applicable)]

SECTION 4 – Potential Gaps / Areas to Verify:
- [Gap 1: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
- [Gap 2: what is missing or unclear, and its impact]
(If no gaps: "No critical gaps identified.")

SECTION 5 – Experience Breakdown:
Years of Experience: [exact number, e.g., "9 years" or "5-6 years"]
Seniority Level: [ENTRY: 0-2yrs | MID: 2-5yrs | SENIOR: 5-10yrs | LEAD: 10-15yrs | EXECUTIVE: 15+yrs]
Core Technical Skills: [List top 10 technical skills from CV]
Secondary Skills: [List additional tools, frameworks, soft skills]
Primary Domain: [Main industry/sector e.g., "Automotive Embedded", "Web Development"]
Leadership Indicators: [List concrete evidence: "Led 5-person team", "Mentored 3 juniors", or "None mentioned"]

FINAL RECOMMENDATION:
[SHORTLIST | BACKUP | REVIEW]
Confidence: [0-100]%
Match Score: [0-100]%

Reason: [2-3 sentences with specific evidence. First sentence: overall verdict with primary reason. Second: key matching evidence. Third: what tips the balance.]

===== DECISION RULES =====
- SHORTLIST: 80%+ requirements matched with strong evidence. Ready for interview.
- BACKUP: 60-79% requirements matched. Good candidate but has some gaps.
- REVIEW: <60% matched OR unclear/insufficient CV data → human must decide.
- If Confidence <70%, automatically use REVIEW regardless of match score.
- NEVER use REJECT — when in doubt, use REVIEW.

---

JOB DESCRIPTION:
Senior Automotive Embedded Software Engineer

MANDATORY REQUIREMENTS (Must Have):
• 5+ years of automotive embedded software development experience
• Expert-level proficiency in Embedded C and C++
• Strong AUTOSAR architecture and integration experience
• Experience with Infineon Aurix or similar automotive microcontrollers
• ISO 26262 functional safety knowledge and application
• Automotive SPICE process experience
• CAN/CAN-FD protocol expertise

NICE TO HAVE (Preferred):
• ISO/SAE 21434 automotive cybersecurity certification
• Electric vehicle or hybrid powertrain experience
• Battery management system (BMS) development
• Vector CANoe, CANalyzer tools
• Requirements engineering with DOORS, PTC Integrity, or CodeBeamer
• Experience with major OEMs (Mercedes, BMW, Volkswagen, etc.)
• Team leadership or technical architect experience
• MISRA C/C++ compliance expertise

ROLE DESCRIPTION:
We are seeking a senior embedded software engineer to lead AUTOSAR architecture design and integration for next-generation electric vehicle platforms. The ideal candidate will work with cross-functional teams to develop safety-critical automotive systems, conduct SPICE assessments, and mentor junior engineers.

Key responsibilities include:
- Designing and implementing AUTOSAR software components
- Conducting requirements engineering for electric powertrain systems
- Performing functional safety analysis per ISO 26262
- Integrating software on Infineon Aurix multicore platforms
- Leading technical reviews and SPICE assessments
- Collaborating with international OEM customers

This role requires strong technical expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work independently in a fast-paced automotive development environment.

---
